diet: Include foods rich in Vitamin A and C, such as carrots, spinach, and citrus fruits.
Consume Triphala powder with honey or ghee at bedtime.
Add cow's ghee to your daily diet to nourish the eyes.
Include amla (Indian gooseberry) in your diet regularly.
Eat handful of soaked almonds daily.
exercise: Palming: Rub hands together to generate heat and place over closed eyes for 2-3 minutes, 3 times a day.
Blinking exercises: Blink rapidly 10 times, close eyes for 20 seconds. Repeat 5 times.
Focus shifting: Shift focus from near to far objects for 5 minutes daily.
Anulom Vilom Pranayama (Alternate Nostril Breathing) for 10-15 minutes daily to improve oxygenation.
OPHTHALMOLOGICAL.how_to_take_products: Take Eye Safe Blend RAS and EXE ALOEVERA SYRUP internally as directed on an empty stomach. EYEDOC DROP should be applied externally into the eyes using a clean dropper, avoiding contact between the dropper and the eye.
things_to_avoid: Avoid excessive exposure to bright sunlight or UV rays without protection.
Avoid prolonged screen time without breaks.
Avoid smoking and excessive alcohol consumption.
Avoid heavy, fried, and extremely spicy foods that aggravate Pitta dosha.
Avoid reading in dim light.
OPHTHALMOLOGICAL.ayurvedic_tip: Washing eyes with Triphala water every morning can help maintain healthy vision and slow down cataract progression.

diet: Consume plenty of dark leafy greens, especially kale and spinach.
Include Vitamin C rich foods to help reduce intraocular pressure.
Drink green tea for its antioxidant properties.
Incorporate foods high in Omega-3 fatty acids, like flaxseeds and walnuts.
Reduce caffeine and high-sodium foods which may spike eye pressure.
exercise: Mild aerobic exercises like walking for 30-40 minutes daily to lower intraocular pressure.
Bhramari Pranayama (Bee Breath) for 5-10 minutes to reduce stress.
Neck rotations and shoulder shrugs to release tension (5 minutes daily).
Avoid exercises where the head is positioned lower than the heart (like headstands).
OPHTHALMOLOGICAL.how_to_take_products: Take IMMUNODOC RAS and EXE ALOEVERA SYRUP in the morning on an empty stomach. Apply EYEDOC DROP gently, ensuring not to rub the eyes vigorously after application.
things_to_avoid: Avoid inverted yoga poses that can increase pressure in the head and eyes.
Avoid heavy weightlifting or straining activities.
Avoid excessive consumption of coffee or caffeinated drinks.
Avoid drinking large amounts of fluid in a short amount of time.
Avoid stress and anger, which aggravate Pitta and Vata.
OPHTHALMOLOGICAL.ayurvedic_tip: Practicing Trataka (steady gazing at a candle flame or a point) can improve concentration and soothe the optic nerve, but should be done gently.

diet: Eat a diet rich in lutein and zeaxanthin (kale, spinach, broccoli).
Include colorful fruits and vegetables (peppers, squash) for antioxidants.
Consume zinc-rich foods like pumpkin seeds and chickpeas.
Include cow's ghee in daily meals to rejuvenate eye tissues (Netra Rasayana).
Consume amla daily for its high Vitamin C content.
exercise: Figure-eight eye exercise: Trace an imaginary figure eight with your eyes for 3-5 minutes daily.
